test.observation: 41 test files ran; all 2819 tests passed, including src/__tests__/cross-area-integration.test.ts and src/__tests__/ShopCloseMerchantDespawn.test.ts.

lint.observation: Build completed successfully via the repo's configured substitute validator; Vite emitted only the existing large-chunk warning after producing dist assets.

blockingIssues.description: The feature required disabling physics on far monsters while keeping AI running, but the implementation instead skips monster.update() entirely for non-aggro monsters beyond 30 tiles. This changes gameplay semantics by freezing far idle monsters and does not implement the requested optimization target.

The new pre-rendered fog renderer is not wired into the actual gameplay scene. ZoneScene continues to use its own exploredTiles/LightingSystem path and never constructs FogOfWarSystem, so the primary fog-rendering optimization described in the feature is not exercised in-game.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that SHOP_CLOSE now carries an optional npcId payload and UIScene tracks shopNpcId so listeners can distinguish which NPC shop actually closed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The systems-worker skill says to write tests first and cover expected behavior, but for scene/rendering fixes the worker ended up using contract-only tests that never exercised the modified ZoneScene/UIScene methods. The skill could explicitly distinguish when scene-level changes require lightweight integration seams or source-level assertions instead of pure module tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: src/__tests__/SpriteBlocksAndMerchant.test.ts only checks drawer keys and EventBus behavior; it does not call `spawnHiddenAreaRewardSprite()`, `handleWanderingMerchantEvent()`, or any UIScene close paths referenced in the feature.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The worker skill says to write tests first and to run full verification cleanly, but the transcript shows implementation edits before tests and records `npx vitest run` as effectively passing despite a known failing test remaining. The skill/procedure may need to be stricter about treating existing failing validators as blockers unless the feature explicitly scopes them out.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: systems-worker SKILL.md steps 3 and 6 vs transcript skeleton for session 5d22c0f4-20ba-4346-8c1f-9fd3e07ed322 and handoff verification noting `2807 tests passed, 1 failed`.

rejectedObservations.observation: A key codebase fact is that `FogOfWarSystem` is currently not used by `ZoneScene`; gameplay fog relies on separate `exploredTiles` and `LightingSystem` logic. That architectural reality was discovered during implementation but is not captured in shared project knowledge, making future fog-related tasks easier to mis-scope.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous; useful architecture fact but not applied automatically during scrutiny because no existing library target was confirmed and this run is failing before follow-up fixes
